channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3184242: Support brotli compression of assets
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 80 files, +198/−612 · php +198/−612
  • Diff: 1f55640
  • Discussion: #3184242 · 16 contributors · 88 comments
  • Followers: 41

Summary

Drupal now generates Brotli-compressed (.br) versions of aggregated CSS and JS assets alongside the existing gzip (.gz) files, provided the PHP brotli extension is installed. The Apache .htaccess rules are updated to serve .br files with priority over .gz when a client advertises brotli support. The system.performance.css.gzip and system.performance.js.gzip config settings are renamed to compress; an automatic upgrade path migrates existing configuration. Sites without the brotli extension see no change in behaviour.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: system_post_update_migrate_compress_setting() migrates css.gzip and js.gzip to css.compress and js.compress
  • Config change: system.performance.css.gzip and system.performance.js.gzip renamed to system.performance.css.compress and system.performance.js.compress
  • Deprecation: system.performance..gzip config keys deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0; use system.performance..compress instead

Technical details

The core change is in AssetDumper::dumpToUri(). Where it previously checked $file_extension . '.gzip' to decide whether to write a .gz file, it now checks the new compress key first, falling back to the deprecated gzip key for backward compatibility. When compression is enabled and extension_loaded('brotli') is true, it writes a .br file via brotli_compress($data, 11, BROTLI_TEXT) after writing the .gz file. The .htaccess in both sites/ and core/ gains RewriteCond/RewriteRule blocks for .css.br and .js.br, placed before the gzip rules so brotli is preferred, and matching FilesMatch blocks set Content-Encoding: br and Vary: Accept-Encoding. The system.performance config schema (in system.schema.yml) gains a new compress key under both css and js; the existing gzip key is kept but marked deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 with removal in drupal:12.0.0. The post-update hook system_post_update_migrate_compress_setting() copies css.gzip → css.compress and js.gzip → js.compress and clears the old keys. A new AssetCompressUpdatePathTest verifies the upgrade path. The design choice to use a single compress boolean rather than separate per-algorithm flags was settled in the issue thread by andypost and catch: because .htaccess rules always check for .br files regardless of a per-algorithm config flag, a finer-grained setting would give a false impression of control while still causing filesystem lookups for non-existent files on servers without brotli.

Upgrade

If custom code reads or writes the old config keys, update references:

// Before
\Drupal::config('system.performance')->get('css.gzip');
\Drupal::config('system.performance')->get('js.gzip');

// After
\Drupal::config('system.performance')->get('css.compress');
\Drupal::config('system.performance')->get('js.compress');

The automatic post-update hook system_post_update_migrate_compress_setting() migrates the values in the database. If you ship system.performance.yml in a recipe or distribution with gzip: true, update it to compress: true before targeting Drupal 12.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 23, 2020
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (5 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • prudloff (Insite)
  • webflo (UEBERBIT GmbH)
  • yogeshmpawar (Portage CyberTech)
  • jnoordsij
  • ravi.shankar (OpenSense Labs)

Collaboration

The design evolved significantly over the thread. The original patch added separate system.performance.css.brotli and system.performance.js.brotli boolean keys alongside gzip. andypost pointed out in comment #15102385 that a single compress key that enables every available algorithm would be cleaner and more future-proof. catch reinforced this in comment #15102469, noting that .htaccess rules cannot be conditionally applied per-algorithm anyway, so a fine-grained config flag would be misleading. prudloff agreed and updated the MR to use the single compress key. Testing was blocked until andypost filed #3512083 to add the brotli PHP extension to the CI Docker images, which unblocked the functional test prudloff had written.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578477: CKEditor 5 should respect <textarea disabled> dynamically
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +81/−10 · php +69/−7 · js +12/−3
  • Diff: 4b4ace1
  • Discussion: #3578477 · 3 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

CKEditor 5 fields that are toggled enabled or disabled at runtime (for example via the Form API #states system or the Conditional Fields module) now correctly enter and leave read-only mode as the disabled attribute changes. Previously the read-only state was evaluated only once when the editor was initialized, so any subsequent enable/disable had no effect on the editor's editability.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The original fix in #3362386 added a one-time check at editor startup: if element.hasAttribute('disabled') was true, editor.enableReadOnlyMode('ckeditor5_disabled') was called and nothing more happened. This issue replaces that one-time check in ckeditor5.js with a MutationObserver that watches for any attribute mutation on the textarea element. On every mutation, a new readOnlyIfDisabled() helper checks element.disabled and calls either editor.enableReadOnlyMode('ckeditor5_disabled') or editor.disableReadOnlyMode('ckeditor5_disabled') accordingly. readOnlyIfDisabled() is also called immediately after the observer is attached to cover the initial state. The test infrastructure in CKEditor5ReadOnlyModeTest is refactored: field creation is extracted into reusable createCkeditorField() and createField() helpers, and three new test fields (field_ckeditor5_disable, field_ckeditor5_enable, field_ckeditor5_toggle) are added. A new testReadOnlyModeDynamic() functional JS test checks and unchecks the toggle and asserts that contenteditable flips appropriately on both the "disable on toggle" and "enable on toggle" fields. The ScriptBytes expectation in AssetAggregationAcrossPagesTest is bumped from 3811015 to 3811582 to account for the slightly larger ckeditor5.js.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 11, 2026
  • Committed: April 27, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • tgauges
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3561960: Combine the database queries in ::loadFromSharedTables() and ::loadFromDedicatedTables()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +137/−79 · php +137/−79
  • Diff: 529ca79
  • Discussion: #3561960 · 4 contributors · 30 comments
  • Followers: 12

Summary

Entity loading in Drupal now uses one fewer database query per load operation. Previously, SqlContentEntityStorage issued one query for the shared data/revision tables and a separate query for dedicated field tables. These two queries are now merged into one, reducing entity loading to at most two or three queries in all cases (down from potentially over a hundred before this and the two preceding issues). Performance tests show 3 to 7 fewer queries per request on various pages, with higher gains on uncached requests where multiple entity loads occur.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: SqlContentEntityStorage::loadFromDedicatedTables() gains a required third parameter array &$translations; subclasses overriding this method must add it
  • Deprecation: SqlContentEntityStorage::loadFromSharedTables() deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0 with no replacement (see https://www.drupal.org/node/3586362)

Technical details

The core change is in SqlContentEntityStorage::mapFromStorageRecords(). The two sequential calls to loadFromSharedTables() and loadFromDedicatedTables() are replaced by a single call to loadFromDedicatedTables(), which now also handles the work that loadFromSharedTables() previously performed.

loadFromDedicatedTables() receives a new third parameter, array &$translations, so it can track language variants as it processes rows. The method builds its base query from the data table or revision table (whichever is appropriate), now selecting all columns from that base table via ->fields($base_table) rather than only the ID. Because the base table and dedicated field tables both have a langcode column, the base table langcode is aliased as {table_name}__langcode (stored in $base_langcode_alias) to prevent it from being overwritten by dedicated table columns in the result set.

For entities that have both a revisionDataTable and a dataTable, the method now also detects fields that are not revisioned (data fields) and, when loading a revision, LEFT JOINs the data table to pick up those values in the same query.

Single cardinality dedicated fields continue to be LEFT JOINed onto this now-expanded base query. The previous validity filter on $langcodes (a pre-fetched list of all language codes) is removed; instead, the langcode of each row is used directly from the aliased column.

loadFromSharedTables() is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:12.0.0 with no replacement, per the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3586362.

This issue is the third in a series: #3551308 merged single cardinality dedicated fields into one query, #3564689 merged multiple cardinality fields into one query, and this issue eliminates the separate shared-table query entirely.

Upgrade

If you have a class that overrides loadFromSharedTables(), remove the override. The method is deprecated and its logic is now handled inside loadFromDedicatedTables().

If you have a class that overrides loadFromDedicatedTables(), add the new third parameter:

// Before
protected function loadFromDedicatedTables(array &$values, $load_from_revision) {

// After
protected function loadFromDedicatedTables(array &$values, $load_from_revision, array &$translations) {

If your override calls parent::loadFromDedicatedTables(), pass $translations through:

parent::loadFromDedicatedTables($values, $load_from_revision, $translations);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 8, 2025
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (4 months and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)

Collaboration

The langcode handling was the main difficulty catch called out explicitly: entities can have base, data, revision, and revision-data tables in various combinations, and the default-language/translation logic differs between shared and dedicated tables. Catch worked through several rounds of failures (MariaDB being the last to pass), noting that the result was not dramatically more complex but was hard to get right. alexpott raised a specific concern during review about the base_langcode alias potentially clashing with a custom entity field named base_langcode, and suggested a mitigation. Catch resolved that with what they described as "one alias rename" before the issue was committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2954834: Add poster image to HTML5 media videos
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +364/−0 · php +355 · yml +8 · txt +1
  • Diff: c9798f8
  • Discussion: #2954834 · 36 contributors · 166 comments
  • Followers: 66

Summary

Site owners can now configure a poster image for HTML5 video fields without any custom code or template overrides. By adding an image field to the entity type that holds the video and selecting it in the "Manage Display" settings for the video formatter, the image is rendered as the HTML5 poster attribute on the <video> element. An image style can optionally be applied to control dimensions. This works on any entity type that has both a video file field and an image field, not only media entities.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New poster and poster_image_style machine_name keys added to the field.formatter.settings.file_video config schema.
  • New API: FileVideoFormatter adds poster and poster_image_style formatter settings with supporting viewElements(), calculateDependencies(), and onDependencyRemoval() overrides; subclasses must account for the new constructor parameters.

Technical details

The FileVideoFormatter class in core/modules/file/src/Plugin/Field/FieldFormatter/FileVideoFormatter.php gains two new settings: poster (the machine name of an image field on the host entity) and poster_image_style (an image style ID, optional). The formatter is converted to use constructor injection, adding AccountInterface, an EntityStorageInterface for image styles, and EntityFieldManagerInterface as constructor arguments alongside the existing parent arguments.

In settingsForm(), EntityFieldManagerInterface::getFieldDefinitions() is called to enumerate image-type fields on the current entity bundle, which are offered as poster candidates via a select element. A poster_image_style select is conditionally shown (using #states) only when a poster field is selected, and includes a "Configure Image Styles" link visible to users with the administer image styles permission. The settingsSummary() output is extended to include poster field and image style selections when set.

In the new viewElements() override, when a poster field is configured and not empty, the code retrieves the first image file item from the host entity, applies a language-aware translation check (TranslatableInterface::hasTranslation()), then either calls ImageStyle::buildUrl() (if a style is configured) or File::createFileUrl() (for the original). A CacheableMetadata bubble is applied for both the image style config entity and the file entity, and the resolved URL is merged onto the video element's #attributes object via Attribute::merge().

calculateDependencies() and onDependencyRemoval() are implemented to track the chosen poster_image_style as a config dependency and substitute a replacement style if the original is deleted.

Config schema at field.formatter.settings.file_video gains two new machine_name keys: poster and poster_image_style, both with requiredKey: false. New functional tests in FileVideoPosterFormatterTest cover both the no-image-style and image-style paths.

Transcript (<track>) support was originally part of this issue but was split into a sibling issue to unblock the more widely needed poster feature. Note that a settingsSummary() guard for a transcript setting appears in the diff as a leftover; since no transcript default or schema key exists, it is inert.

Upgrade

If you subclass FileVideoFormatter, update your constructor to accept and pass through the three new injected services:

public function __construct(
  $plugin_id,
  $plugin_definition,
  FieldDefinitionInterface $field_definition,
  array $settings,
  $label,
  $view_mode,
  array $third_party_settings,
  AccountInterface $currentUser,
  EntityStorageInterface $imageStyleStorage,
  EntityFieldManagerInterface $entityFieldManager,
) {
  parent::__construct(...);
}

public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, ...) {
  return new static(
    ...,
    $container->get('current_user'),
    $container->get('entity_type.manager')->getStorage('image_style'),
    $container->get('entity_field.manager'),
  );
}

Existing display configurations using the file_video formatter will not have the poster or poster_image_style keys in their stored config. They default to empty strings at runtime, so existing video rendering is unaffected, but if you export configuration you may see these keys appear when the display is next saved.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 21, 2018
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (8 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • bailleux
  • seanb
  • joel_osc (OpenPlus)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • mistrae
  • sleitner
  • pbouchereau
  • anybody (DROWL.de)
  • grevil (DROWL.de)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • dhirendra.mishra (Valuebound)
  • bs pavan (Material)
  • martijn de wit (Ablerz)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

Collaboration

The issue sat dormant for five years after the original feature request in 2018. seanb made the key early design observation that the poster image field should live on the parent entity and be mapped in the formatter, making it work beyond media entities. anybody broke the stalemate in May 2023 by converting the accumulated patch work into MR !4010, adding image style support, and getting the feature to a functional state. catch then guided two important decisions: using hook_entity_presave() and hook_post_update_NAME() for the config upgrade path, and splitting the transcript feature out into a separate issue (#3372592) so the simpler poster-only change was not blocked by unresolved <track> semantics. grevil created the final 11.x MR (!4046) after anybody's MR could not be rebased automatically.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581434: Entity serialization issue in Layout Builder due to content moderation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +115/−6 · php +115/−6
  • Diff: 117fee7
  • Discussion: #3581434 · 4 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Sites using Layout Builder with content moderation and multiple translations could hit a fatal serialization error when editing node layouts. The error would surface in PrepareLayout::onPrepareLayout() when Drupal tried to store the section storage to the layout temporary storage. This bug is fixed in 11.x and 11.3.x. No configuration or code changes are required for site owners.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is a broken reference in ContentEntityBase::__clone(). When a translation is initialized via initializeTranslation(), the $originalEntity property is turned into a PHP reference shared between the entity and all its translations, keeping them in sync. This is the same pattern used for several other properties (enforceIsNew, newRevision, fields, etc.). For all those other properties, __clone() explicitly breaks the reference by reassigning a local copy. However, $originalEntity was omitted from that list (the handling was removed during the original work in #2839195 without explanation).

When ContentEntityStorageBase::createRevision() clones an entity (as triggered by ContentModeration\EntityTypeInfo::entityPrepareForm()), the clone inherits the shared reference. When content moderation then calls setOriginal() on the draft, the value propagates via the still-live reference to every other entity that shares it. Worse, because the entity being set as the original is itself one of the objects sharing the reference, $this->originalEntity ends up pointing to the entity itself, creating a circular self-reference that breaks PHP serialization.

The fix is a three-line addition to ContentEntityBase::__clone() that follows the exact same pattern already in place for the other properties:

$originalEntity = $this->originalEntity;
$this->originalEntity = &$originalEntity;

This breaks the shared reference so that post-clone changes to $originalEntity on one object do not propagate to others. ContentEntityCloneTest received a new dedicated testEntityOriginal() method that covers the translation-sharing behavior, the clone-isolation requirement, and the merged-revision-original scenario where createRevision() sets an original automatically. The existing skip of the originalEntity property in testEntityPropertiesModifications() was updated to point to this new test instead.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 25, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • matthijs (Dropsolid)
  • webflo (UEBERBIT GmbH)
  • tstoeckler (resc.)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

tstoeckler diagnosed the true root cause in depth after discussing the problem with webflo at DrupalDevDays Athens. webflo had already filed a first MR (#15475) targeting ContentEntityStorageBase::createRevision(), but tstoeckler traced the bug further back to the missing __clone() handling and opened MR #15554 with the minimal, correct fix. tstoeckler also wrote a thorough test and noted an adjacent issue worth addressing separately, which was subsequently filed as #3586860. webflo confirmed the fix resolved the problem on their production site.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3566351: Throwing AccessDeniedException without a route causes PHP errors
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +41/−1 · php +41/−1
  • Diff: 051f438
  • Discussion: #3566351 · 5 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

When contrib modules or event subscribers throw an AccessDeniedException before a route has been matched (for example in a KernelEvents::REQUEST subscriber), Drupal would produce a fatal "Call to a member function hasRequirement() on null" error instead of a clean 403 response. The fix is now in Drupal 11.x and 11.3.x.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The crash occurred in CsrfExceptionSubscriber::on403() in core/lib/Drupal/Core/EventSubscriber/CsrfExceptionSubscriber.php. The method retrieved the route via RouteMatch::createFromRequest($request)->getRouteObject(), which returns null when no route has been matched, then immediately called $route->hasRequirement('_csrf_token') without a null check. The fix adds is_null($route) as the first condition in the guard clause so the method returns early when there is no matched route. A new unit test CsrfExceptionSubscriberTest::testOn403WithNullRouteDoesNothing() was added in core/tests/Drupal/Tests/Core/EventSubscriber/CsrfExceptionSubscriberTest.php to cover this path. During the review, a concern was raised that the fix could create an infinite 403-redirect loop when the CSRF confirm form route itself triggers a 403; the null-route guard addresses the reported crash without touching the redirect logic.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 8, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (3 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • rgpublic
  • sourav_paul
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

sourav_paul caught a potential infinite redirect loop before the MR was filed: with a naive earlier patch, a 403 on a CSRF-protected route could redirect to _csrf_confirm_form_route, which could trigger another 403, and so on until memory was exhausted. rgpublic proposed adding a !$event->isMainRequest() guard to break the cycle. The final MR, opened by sourav_paul, uses the simpler is_null($route) guard that fixes the reported null-dereference crash without altering the redirect behaviour.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585498: Alter hook implementations in Kernel test classes are not executed
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +73/−2 · php +73/−2
  • Diff: 849bda6
  • Discussion: #3585498 · 3 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Alter hook implementations defined directly inside Kernel test classes were silently skipped whenever ModuleHandler::alter() was called with multiple related hook types (for example ['form', 'form_my_form']). The bug affected only test code, not production sites. After this fix, Kernel test classes can reliably implement alter hooks alongside regular hooks without needing a separate test module.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Issue #3502432 introduced the ability to write hook implementations as methods on a KernelTestBase subclass. Those implementations are registered under the pseudo-module key 'core'. When ModuleHandler::alter() is invoked with more than one hook type, it calls reOrderModulesForAlter() to produce a canonical ordering. That method computed array_intersect(array_keys($this->moduleList), $modules), which filtered out any module name not in the installed module list. Because 'core' is never in $this->moduleList, every alter hook implemented on a Kernel test class was silently dropped from the combined listener list and never called.

The fix changes the expression to array_intersect(array_keys($this->moduleList + ['core' => '']), $modules). Adding 'core' as a trailing entry in the array union means it survives the intersect while still sorting last, matching the order that KernelTestCompilerPass uses when registering test hook implementations. A new test class KernelTestHooksTest (in core/tests/Drupal/KernelTests/Core/Hook/) verifies that both regular and alter hook methods on a Kernel test class are executed correctly, including the multi-type alter case that previously failed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 17, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (10 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3584324: Make the chained fast backend self-heal when last_write_timestamp is missing
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +60/−0 · php +60
  • Diff: c3a6c1a
  • Discussion: #3584324 · 5 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The ChainedFastBackend cache class had a gap in its self-healing logic: if the last_write_timestamp entry was absent from the persistent (consistent) backend, cache reads would fall through to the persistent backend and write items back to the fast backend, but those fast-backend entries could never be validated because no timestamp existed to compare against. The situation would persist silently until an actual cache miss triggered a genuine set(), which finally established the timestamp. The fix writes a fresh timestamp immediately when the missing condition is detected, so the fast backend becomes usable right away.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In ChainedFastBackend::getMultiple(), after the existing call to $this->getLastWriteTimestamp(), a new guard if ($last_write_timestamp === 0) calls $this->markAsOutdated(). markAsOutdated() writes a current-time value to the persistent backend under the last_write_timestamp key, which makes subsequent fast-backend writes valid immediately. The condition === 0 is the correct test because getLastWriteTimestamp() returns 0 when no entry exists. Dries caught an early version of the patch that had the condition wrong, and catch corrected it. The missing-timestamp scenario can arise in at least two ways: (1) converting an existing cache bin to chained fast while leaving warm persistent-cache entries in place, so the persistent backend has data but no timestamp; (2) LRU eviction on a backend like Redis or Memcache that removes the timestamp key while retaining other cache items. A new unit test testLastWriteTimestampMissing() in ChainedFastBackendTest covers the scenario by mocking both backends, asserting that set() is called once on the consistent backend (for the timestamp) and once on the fast backend (for the cache item). An existing test testGetDoesNotHitConsistentBackend() also gained an assertion that set() is never called on the consistent backend in the normal warm-cache path. Discovered while investigating #3583040 (Cache the container definition in APCu).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 12, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (16 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dries
  • kristiaanvandeneynde (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

Dries reviewed the patch and identified that the condition on the timestamp check was inverted, pointing out it should be if ($last_write_timestamp === 0). catch confirmed the mistake and fixed it, also adding an additional expectation to an existing test that would have caught the error. kristiaanvandeneynde raised a useful question about how often this situation can realistically arise; catch explained the two concrete scenarios (converting a bin to chained fast with warm data, and LRU eviction of the timestamp key) and noted the worst visible symptom is excess APCu writes rather than outright breakage.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #1797438: HTML5 validation is preventing form submit and not fully accessible
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +283/−179 · php +283/−179
  • Diff: d5babbb
  • Discussion: #1797438 · 68 contributors · 303 comments
  • Followers: 142

Summary

Since Drupal 8, forms have carried native HTML5 required and constraint attributes that cause browsers to intercept form submissions client-side before Drupal's Form API (FAPI) ever runs. The result is generic, browser-localized error messages ("Please fill out this field.") instead of Drupal's descriptive, accessible FAPI messages. Only the first invalid field surfaces an error, and screen readers handle this poorly. This change introduces a new enable_html5_validation setting in settings.php so site owners can disable browser-native HTML5 validation and rely on Drupal's server-side validation messages instead. In Drupal 11 the setting defaults to enabled, with a status report warning prompting admins to prepare. In Drupal 12 the default changes to disabled; in Drupal 13 the setting will be removed and HTML5 validation will always be off.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: New enable_html5_validation setting in settings.php controls whether browser-native HTML5 form validation is active; defaults to TRUE in Drupal 11, FALSE in Drupal 12, and will be removed in Drupal 13.

Technical details

The root problem is that the HTML5 required attribute (and other constraint attributes like type="email") causes browsers to block form submission before FAPI runs. Browser validation messages are inconsistent: they only report the first invalid field, use the browser's locale rather than Drupal's language, and are handled poorly by screen readers (screen reader testing confirmed JAWS reads only "Alert: Please fill out this field" regardless of field name, while VoiceOver on Safari reads nothing at all). The #limit_validation_errors use case was also affected, though the existing formnovalidate attribute on affected buttons already partially mitigated that.

The fix adds a Settings::get('enable_html5_validation', TRUE) check inside preprocessForm() in the form element preprocessor. When the setting is FALSE, a novalidate attribute is added to the rendered <form> element, disabling all browser HTML5 validation globally. A documentation block and commented-out example (# $settings['enable_html5_validation'] = TRUE;) are added to default/settings.php. The SystemManager::checkRequirements() method gains a new status-report check: when the setting is NULL (not configured), it warns that Drupal 12 will default to FALSE and advises testing; when the setting is TRUE, it warns that the setting will be removed in Drupal 13. No warning is shown when the setting is FALSE.

Three form validation tests (testValidateLimitErrors, testPatternValidation, testCustomRequiredError) are moved from a non-JS functional test class to a new FunctionalJavascript test class (Drupal\Tests\system\FunctionalJavascript\Form\ValidationTest). The new class sets enable_html5_validation to FALSE in setUp() via writeSettings(), ensuring the tests run in a real browser environment with HTML5 validation disabled so FAPI messages are testable.

Upgrade

To disable HTML5 validation on all forms and use Drupal's FAPI validation messages, add the following to settings.php:

$settings['enable_html5_validation'] = FALSE;

To explicitly keep the current behavior (HTML5 validation on) and suppress the status report warning in Drupal 11, set:

$settings['enable_html5_validation'] = TRUE;

Note: setting TRUE will display a different status report warning reminding you to prepare for removal in Drupal 13. The setting will be removed entirely in Drupal 13, at which point HTML5 validation will always be disabled.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 28, 2012
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (13 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • Bojhan
  • xjm (Acquia)
  • nod_ (Smile)
  • mgifford (CivicActions)
  • webchick
  • sun
  • ParisLiakos
  • catch
  • dcam
  • jessebeach
  • cosmicdreams
  • vprocessor (Skilld)
  • skaught
  • idebr (ezCompany)
  • jefuri (Synetic)
  • dmsmidt (Synetic)
  • lauriii (Druid)
  • GrandmaGlassesRopeMan (Acquia)
  • andrewmacpherson (Annertech)
  • fenstrat (PreviousNext)
  • sja112 (Material)
  • ankiitsinghh
  • pameeela
  • tanubansal (Salsa Digital)
  • himanshu_jhaloya (Valuebound)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • Everett Zufelt
  • tim.plunkett
  • gaurav.kapoor (OpenSense Labs)
  • geek-merlin (MachbarMacher)
  • kieran.cott
  • suresh prabhu parkala (Specbee)
  • prudloff (Insite)
  • kentr
  • jcandan
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3580855: Entity recursive rendering protection is not compatible with Fibers
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +174/−0 · php +163 · yml +11
  • Diff: a2ee249
  • Discussion: #3580855 · 5 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

When BigPipe renders blocks as placeholders, each block runs inside a PHP Fiber. A bug in the entity recursive-rendering protection introduced by #2940605 caused recursion keys to be shared across Fibers. If two blocks on the same page rendered the same entity, the second Fiber would see the first Fiber's recursion key, treat it as an infinite-loop guard, and suppress the entity's output entirely. The fix scopes recursion keys per Fiber so each block renders independently. Sites using BigPipe with multiple blocks that display the same entity (for example, menu blocks showing node data) were most likely to encounter missing content.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The entity recursive-rendering protection in EntityViewBuilder::getRenderRecursionKey() builds a string key from the entity's class, object ID, and optionally its language. This key is stored in a static tracking array to prevent infinite recursion when an entity renders itself again mid-build. Because PHP Fibers are cooperative coroutines rather than true threads, multiple in-flight Fibers share the same static state. When BigPipe placeholder rendering suspends Fiber A mid-render (for example, while a formatted text field's filter placeholders are resolved) and starts Fiber B for a second block rendering the same entity, Fiber B finds Fiber A's recursion key already present and incorrectly treats it as recursion, returning an empty render array. The fix adds a Fiber-specific component to the key: when \Fiber::getCurrent() is non-null, spl_object_id($fiber) is appended to $recursion_keys, making every Fiber's keys distinct. A new functional test EntityConcurrentRenderTest verifies the scenario: two entity_test_block blocks (which force createPlaceholder(): true) both render the same entity that has a formatted text field; the filter placeholder creation is what causes each Fiber to suspend and allow interleaving. The bug was introduced by #2940605 and had not yet shipped in a stable release, so no backport to 11.3.x was required.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 23, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • taran2l (Lemberg Solutions)
  • godotislate
  • amateescu
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

amateescu identified that the same bug was responsible for the random test failures tracked in #3583187 and then wrote the functional test for this fix. In their words, it was "quite tricky to determine all the conditions required to hit this problem": the block must be placeholdered so it renders in a Fiber, and the entity must have a formatted text field so that filter placeholder creation causes the Fiber to suspend mid-render, allowing the second block's Fiber to start and collide on the shared recursion key.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579922: Add missing type hint for $variables parameter in theme hooks
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +34/−34 · php +34/−34
  • Diff: 387533d
  • Discussion: #3579922 · 6 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Several hook implementation methods in the Olivero theme's OliveroHooks class were missing explicit array type declarations on their by-reference parameters. This is an internal code quality improvement with no user-facing effect, aligning Olivero with Drupal's move toward stricter typing in PHP. The scope was expanded during review to include all affected parameters in the class rather than fixing them one hook at a time.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: 27 method signatures in OliveroHooks now declare array on their by-reference parameters (e.g. array &$variables); code that extends OliveroHooks and overrides these methods must add matching type declarations.

Technical details

In core/themes/olivero/src/Hook/OliveroHooks.php, 27 hook implementation methods had their leading by-reference parameter declared without an explicit type, for example &$variables, &$form, &$suggestions, and &$info. The fix adds the array type hint to each, producing signatures such as array &$variables, array &$form, array &$suggestions, and array &$info. Affected methods include all preprocess_* implementations, both form_alter and form_node_preview_form_select_alter, element_info_alter, theme_suggestions_menu_alter, theme_suggestions_block_alter, theme_suggestions_user_alter, and form_views_exposed_form_alter. The change was originally scoped to Olivero alone; a companion issue #3579925 (ClaroHooks.php) was closed as a duplicate once its changes were cherry-picked into this branch. The issue was blocked briefly on #3579903 (eliminating Olivero's legacy .theme file), which needed to land first so the hook implementations existed only in the OOP OliveroHooks class.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 18, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • sivaji_ganesh_jojodae
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3474070: Allow StarterKit forked themes with prefix
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +147/−10 · php +147/−10
  • Diff: 95b8775
  • Discussion: #3474070 · 7 contributors · 27 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

The drupal generate-theme command would produce garbled file names and content when the starterkit theme's machine name was short enough to appear as a substring in its own derived naming variants. For example, generating a new theme named galactus_ta from a starterkit named galactus resulted in files named galactusTaTa_ta.info.yml instead of galactus_ta.info.yml, ultimately crashing with a PHP fatal error. This fix corrects the replacement logic so theme generation works correctly regardless of the starterkit's machine name length or complexity.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was in GenerateTheme::execute(), which called PHP's str_replace() with two parallel arrays built by namePatterns(). That method produces eight variants per name (plain machine name, camelCase, PascalCase, and title case, for both machine name and label). PHP processes array-based str_replace sequentially, so when the starterkit name was short and lowercase (e.g. galactus), its camelCase variant was identical to the plain name. The first substitution correctly turned galactus into galactus_ta, but the very next pass then found galactus again inside the already-substituted string and transformed it into galactusTa_ta, corrupting every file name and piece of content in the generated theme.

The fix introduces a two-pass placeholder strategy in both the file-content loop and the file-rename loop. Pass 1 replaces every old pattern with a unique, collision-free sentinel string (__MACHINE_NAME__, __MACHINE_NAME_CAMEL__, etc.); Pass 2 replaces those sentinels with the new pattern values. Because no sentinel matches another old pattern, cascading substitutions cannot occur.

Alongside the two-pass change, the diff introduces explicit inline marker comments for starterkit template files: #@starterkit:machine_name, #@starterkit:label, #@starterkit:machine_class_name, and #@starterkit:label_class_name. A preg_replace pass after the generic substitution finds these markers and replaces the line immediately following each one with the correct new value, providing an unambiguous escape hatch when short or identical-looking label fragments would still be ambiguous after the placeholder pass. Existing test fixtures are updated from the old # machine_name comment style to the new #@starterkit: format. A new test testSimpleStarterkitTheme() in GenerateThemeTest covers the short-name scenario end-to-end using a starterkit named simple. The pattern collision was introduced in #3364885, which added namePatterns() and the array-based str_replace.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 13, 2024
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • joelpittet (The University of British Columbia)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • garphy (Evolving Web)
  • yannickoo (1xINTERNET)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

joelpittet filed the issue and did the detailed forensic work, breaking down exactly how each filename was being mutated through the sequential str_replace array and posting the full before/after table in comments. garphy later contributed a patch to combine the two content-replacement passes into one call, avoiding an intermediate file_put_contents. joelpittet eventually found the placeholder approach that made the tests pass after a lengthy period away from the problem, and smustgrave provided manual testing confirmation before the final RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585505: Refactor tests code via Rector
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 100 files, +194/−187 · php +194/−187
  • Diff: 95df9e9
  • Discussion: #3585505 · 4 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

This is a purely internal cleanup of Drupal core's test code with no user-facing effect. It uses the Rector static analysis tool to automatically add PHP type declarations across all test classes in core and core modules, improving code clarity and helping static analysis tools catch bugs earlier.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change applies Rector's typeDeclarations prepared set to every test class under core/tests and core/modules/**/tests/src, covering unit, kernel, functional, and functional-JavaScript tests. The TypedPropertyFromCreateMockAssignRector rule was explicitly excluded from the run because it requires manual adjustment beyond what automation can safely handle.

The mechanical changes fall into four repeating patterns visible throughout the diff:

  1. Return type declarations added to data-provider methods (the vast majority): untyped public static function providerFoo() becomes public static function providerFoo(): array, or : \Iterator where a generator is returned.
  2. Parameter type declarations added to #[DataProvider]-driven test methods: previously untyped parameters like $expected, $route, $method, $state, etc. are given concrete scalar or class types (e.g. string, bool, int, array).
  3. Return and parameter types added to anonymous closures and arrow functions used inline, e.g. function ($v) becoming function (ConstraintViolationInterface $v): string.
  4. Return types added to helper and trait methods in test classes, e.g. : void, : bool, : string|false, : mixed, : BigPipeResponseAttachmentsProcessor.

As a side effect, Rector also alphabetically reordered some use import statements and added a few missing use declarations (e.g. NodeElement in a CKEditor5 test, ConstraintViolationInterface in the CKEditor5 unit test). The PHPStan baseline was regenerated after the run to reflect the new type information.

The follow-up issue #3587327 was filed to run the same process again once new code has been added.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 17, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (10 days later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3580545: Make empty route lookup cacheable
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +344/−10 · php +342/−8 · yml +2/−2
  • Diff: 01c0bc5
  • Discussion: #3580545 · 5 contributors · 33 comments
  • Followers: 14

Summary

When Drupal's routing system could not find a route for a requested path, the resulting 404 response was incorrectly marked as uncacheable (max-age 0). This was a precondition blocker for #2352009, which aims to let the page cache properly respect response cacheability. This fix makes empty route lookups cacheable so that 404 responses now carry max-age -1 (Permanent) in cache debug headers. No additional caching actually occurs from this fix alone, because Dynamic Page Cache operates per-route and cannot act before routing, and the page cache's handling of the max-age is addressed separately in #2352009.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: The Symfony welcome page is no longer triggered when a controller throws new NotFoundHttpException(previous: new NoConfigurationException()); Drupal's regular 404 page is rendered instead.
  • New API: New CacheableResourceNotFoundException in Drupal\Core\Routing\Exception; new Drupal\Core\Http\EventListener\RouterListener replaces Symfony\Component\HttpKernel\EventListener\RouterListener as the router_listener service.

Technical details

The root cause was that Symfony's ResourceNotFoundException does not implement CacheableDependencyInterface. When Router::matchRequest() threw it for an unmatched path, the exception bubbled through DefaultExceptionHtmlSubscriber::makeSubrequest(), which read cacheability off the exception and got nothing, effectively contributing a max-age of 0 to the response.

The fix introduces three coordinated changes. First, a new CacheableResourceNotFoundException class in Drupal\Core\Routing\Exception extends Symfony's ResourceNotFoundException and implements CacheableDependencyInterface via CacheableDependencyTrait. It is constructed with a CacheableMetadata carrying no contexts, no tags, and Cache::PERMANENT max-age. Second, Router::matchRequest() now throws CacheableResourceNotFoundException instead of the bare ResourceNotFoundException when the initial route collection is empty. Third, a new Drupal-owned RouterListener in Drupal\Core\Http\EventListener replaces Symfony\Component\HttpKernel\EventListener\RouterListener (which is declared final in Symfony, so it is copied rather than extended). The key addition in Drupal's RouterListener::onKernelRequest() is a dedicated catch (CacheableResourceNotFoundException) block that wraps the exception in a CacheableNotFoundHttpException, preserving the cacheability metadata so it propagates correctly. The existing catch (ResourceNotFoundException) block remains to handle any non-cacheable cases. The Drupal RouterListener also omits Symfony's welcome-page logic, logging support, and URL-matcher support, which are not applicable in Drupal.

The router_listener service in core.services.yml is updated from Symfony\Component\HttpKernel\EventListener\RouterListener to Drupal\Core\Http\EventListener\RouterListener, removing the previously passed NULL logger argument. Tests in PageCacheTest, the REST module base test class, and a new RouterTest kernel test verify the new behavior and that the thrown exception carries the expected empty cache metadata with permanent max-age.

Upgrade

If your code catches Symfony\Component\Routing\Exception\ResourceNotFoundException thrown by Router::matchRequest(), ensure you also handle (or widen the catch to) Drupal\Core\Routing\Exception\CacheableResourceNotFoundException, since that is now thrown for the empty-route case.

If you depend on the Symfony welcome page appearing when a controller throws:

throw new NotFoundHttpException(previous: new NoConfigurationException());

that behavior is removed. The regular Drupal 404 page is rendered instead.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 20, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • tstoeckler (resc.)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

tstoeckler's initial MR included significant refactoring of RouteProvider to expose CacheableMetadata through route collection lookups, including removing internal methods like getRouteCollectionCacheId() and injecting CacheContextsManager. berdir pointed out in comment #7 that this would cause widespread breakage in contrib modules that override RouteProvider (such as domain_source), and asked whether a less invasive intermediate step was possible. After discussion, tstoeckler reverted the RouteProvider changes and implemented the simpler approach in the final MR, focusing only on the exception chain. The Dynamic Page Cache header-leakage side effect that emerged during review was spun off to the pre-existing #3451483.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574727: Deprecate remaining language.module code
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 15 files, +226/−144 · php +226/−136 · yml +0/−8
  • Diff: a0dcfd3
  • Discussion: #3574727 · 5 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Five procedural functions remaining in language.module have been deprecated as part of the ongoing effort to move Drupal core away from procedural code toward object-oriented hook classes and services. Site owners and module authors who call any of these functions directly will begin receiving deprecation warnings in Drupal 11.4 and should update their code to use the replacement APIs before Drupal 12 or 13, depending on the specific function.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Five procedural language.module functions deprecated in 11.4.0: language_process_language_select (removed in 12.0.0), language_configuration_element_submit, language_get_default_langcode, language_negotiation_url_prefixes_update, and language_get_browser_drupal_langcode_mappings (all removed in 13.0.0); DefaultLanguageItem::getDefaultLangcode() also deprecated (removed in 13.0.0); LanguageMapper constructor without ConfigFactoryInterface deprecated (required in 12.0.0).
  • New API: New LanguageConfiguration::submit() static method on the language configuration form element class; new LanguageHooks::processLanguageSelect() instance method.

Technical details

The issue targeted five procedural functions in language.module that had not yet been converted:

  1. language_process_language_select() — deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0. The logic moved into LanguageHooks::processLanguageSelect() as an instance method. The elementInfoAlter hook method in LanguageHooks now references the callback as static::class . ':processLanguageSelect' and also switches the adjacent Select::processSelect and RenderElementBase::processAjaxForm callbacks from string literals to class-constant form.

  2. language_configuration_element_submit() — deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0. The logic moved into a new static LanguageConfiguration::submit() method on the Drupal\language\Element\LanguageConfiguration form element class. All three callers in ContentEntityForm, NodeForm, and LanguageConfigurationElement test form were updated to reference LanguageConfiguration::class . '::submit' instead of the function name string.

  3. language_get_default_langcode() — deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0, with no public replacement. The logic moved into DefaultLanguageItem::getDefaultLanguageCode() as a protected method. The existing public DefaultLanguageItem::getDefaultLangcode(EntityInterface $entity) method is also now deprecated (removed in drupal:13.0.0) and simply delegates to the new protected method.

  4. language_negotiation_url_prefixes_update() — deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0, with no public replacement. The logic moved into ConfigurableLanguage::updateUrlPrefixes() as a protected method, called from ConfigurableLanguage::postSave(). A comment in LanguageNegotiationUrlSubscriber::onConfigSave() was updated to reference the new method name.

  5. language_get_browser_drupal_langcode_mappings() — deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0, with no public replacement. The sole non-test caller, LanguageMapper::getMappings(), now reads language.mappings configuration directly via a newly injected ConfigFactoryInterface. The LanguageMapper constructor gains an optional ?ConfigFactoryInterface $config_factory parameter (with a @trigger_error deprecation if omitted, required in drupal:12.0.0).

Tests were refactored alongside: the testDefaultLangcode test no longer calls the deprecated function; instead it creates an EntityTest entity and reads langcode->value through DefaultLanguageItem. A separate test form class (LanguageConfigurationElementTest) and its route were removed as they existed only to test the deprecated function via a browser request; that scenario is now covered by a direct entity assertion.

Upgrade

If you call any of these functions directly, replace them as follows:

// language_process_language_select() — removed in drupal:12.0.0
// Before:
'language_process_language_select'
// After (inject or retrieve the LanguageHooks service):
\Drupal\language\Hook\LanguageHooks::class . ':processLanguageSelect'

// language_configuration_element_submit() — removed in drupal:13.0.0
// Before:
$form['#submit'][] = 'language_configuration_element_submit';
// After:
$form['#submit'][] = \Drupal\language\Element\LanguageConfiguration::class . '::submit';

// language_get_default_langcode() — removed in drupal:13.0.0
// No public replacement; read the language configuration directly:
$config = \Drupal\language\Entity\ContentLanguageSettings::loadByEntityTypeBundle($entity_type, $bundle);
$langcode = $config->getDefaultLangcode();

// language_negotiation_url_prefixes_update() — removed in drupal:13.0.0
// No public replacement; the update now happens internally in
// ConfigurableLanguage::postSave().

// language_get_browser_drupal_langcode_mappings() — removed in drupal:13.0.0
// No public replacement; read config directly:
$map = \Drupal::config('language.mappings')->get('map');

If you extend LanguageMapper and rely on its constructor signature, add ConfigFactoryInterface as the fourth argument:

// Before (triggers deprecation in 11.4, breaks in 12.0):
new LanguageMapper($persistent_cache, $memory_cache, $moduleHandler);

// After:
new LanguageMapper($persistent_cache, $memory_cache, $moduleHandler, $configFactory);

If you call DefaultLanguageItem::getDefaultLangcode(EntityInterface $entity) directly, note it is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0 with no public replacement.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 20, 2026
  • Committed: April 28, 2026 (2 months and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

A discussion arose between nicxvan, berdir, and penyaskito (the new language.module maintainer) over two design questions. Berdir raised whether new methods should be consistently targeted at drupal:13.0.0 for removal. Penyaskito confirmed that the drupal:12.0.0 deprecation train had already passed, so only functions already slated for 12.0.0 removal kept that target. Penyaskito also suggested making the new helper methods private to retain more refactoring freedom before Drupal 13. nicxvan pushed back, citing the BC policy that protected methods on plugins are not considered API and that private should be reserved for experimental features. Penyaskito agreed that this was not the right issue to change that policy, gave a +1, and the protected approach was kept.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3527914: Use UMD installation method for CKEditor5
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3527914 · 9 contributors · 47 comments
  • Followers: 28

Summary

CKEditor 5's DLL (Dynamic Link Library) build method is being discontinued: CKEditor 5 v48 already removed DLL support from its published npm packages, making an upgrade impossible without switching build methods. Drupal 12 now switches to the UMD (Universal Module Definition) build method, which ships all CKEditor 5 functionality as a single aggregated file loaded into a CKEDITOR global, along with consolidated per-language translation files. This change lands in Drupal 12 only; Drupal 11 retains the DLL build because CKSource confirmed continued LTS support for the version bundled with Drupal 11 through its end-of-life. Contrib modules that ship CKEditor 5 plugins will need a new major version to support Drupal 12, but a backward-compatibility shim eases the migration.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Contrib CKEditor 5 plugins must replace webpack DllReferencePlugin with externals mapping ckeditor5/@ckeditor/* imports to the CKEDITOR UMD global; per-plugin library declarations should depend on core/ckeditor5 instead of the deprecated stub libraries.
  • Deprecation: Per-plugin stub libraries (core/ckeditor5.autoformat, core/ckeditor5.basic, and ~23 others) deprecated in drupal:12.0.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0; ckeditor5-dll-shim.js (and CKEditor5.dll()) deprecated in drupal:12.0.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0.

Technical details

The DLL build exposed each plugin as a separate JavaScript file that delegated to CKEditor5.dll via webpack's DllReferencePlugin. The UMD build replaces all those individual files with a single core/assets/vendor/ckeditor5/ckeditor5.umd.js that exports everything through the CKEDITOR global. Translation files similarly consolidated from one file per plugin per language to one file per language under core/assets/vendor/ckeditor5/translations/*.umd.js.

On the Drupal side, core.libraries.yml gains the top-level ckeditor5 library entry pointing at ckeditor5.umd.js. The dozens of old per-plugin stub libraries (core/ckeditor5.autoformat, core/ckeditor5.basic, core/ckeditor5.essentials, etc.) are deprecated in drupal:12.0.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; they now simply redirect to core/ckeditor5. Ckeditor5Hooks.php and LanguageMapper.php already looked for .umd.js files when scanning for translation assets, wiring the new consolidated translation files into the library system.

Drupal's own internal plugins under js/ckeditor5_plugins/ are still built separately with webpack, but webpack.config.js now uses an externals callback that maps all ckeditor5 and @ckeditor/* imports to the CKEDITOR global instead of the old DllReferencePlugin approach.

For backward compatibility, a new js/ckeditor5-dll-shim.js is included in the internal.drupal.ckeditor5 library. It sets window.CKEditor5.dll to a function that returns the CKEDITOR global (since the UMD build is flat), but fires a Drupal.deprecationError() on every invocation. This lets many existing DLL-built contrib plugins continue to load in Drupal 12 while triggering console deprecation notices. The shim is deprecated in drupal:12.0.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0.

The decision to use UMD rather than ESM with import maps (which had been the originally planned path, blocked on #3398525) was made because UMD requires no additional platform API work and the DLL EOL deadline had become urgent after CKEditor 5 v48 removed DLL support entirely.

Upgrade

Contrib modules that ship CKEditor 5 plugins built with webpack's DllReferencePlugin need to switch to webpack externals for Drupal 12 compatibility. Replace the DllReferencePlugin configuration with an externals entry that maps ckeditor5 and @ckeditor/* imports to the CKEDITOR global:

// webpack.config.js (Drupal 12 / UMD approach)
externals: [
  function ({ request }, callback) {
    if (request === 'ckeditor5' || request.startsWith('ckeditor5/')) {
      return callback(null, 'CKEDITOR');
    }
    if (request.startsWith('@ckeditor/')) {
      return callback(null, 'CKEDITOR');
    }
    callback();
  },
],

Plugin source files can keep their existing import statements from ckeditor5 or @ckeditor/* packages; only the webpack build configuration changes. Libraries that previously declared a dependency on a now-deprecated stub library (e.g. core/ckeditor5.basic) should be updated to depend on core/ckeditor5 directly. See the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3581531 for full details.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 2, 2025
  • Committed: April 27, 2026 (10 months later)

Collaboration

longwave recognized that the looming DLL EOL made the UMD path the only viable option before the Drupal 12 release window, bypassing the previously blocking import-maps work (#3398525). After opening MR !15208, they also developed the ckeditor5-dll-shim.js backward-compatibility layer after manually verifying it allowed the ckeditor5_plugin_pack contrib module to work on Umami with only a version-requirement bump, giving contrib a softer migration path. The decision to restrict the change to Drupal 12 only followed a direct Slack confirmation from CKSource's Witek Socha that DLL LTS support extends through Drupal 11's end-of-life.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3571172: Deprecate system_sort_themes() and system_check_directory() in system module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3571172 · 4 contributors · 46 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Two procedural functions in system.module have been deprecated as part of an ongoing effort to reduce the system module's footprint. system_sort_themes() is deprecated with no replacement; its only core caller (SystemController::themesPage()) now uses an inlined static closure. system_check_directory(), a form after_build callback, is also deprecated with no replacement; its sole core usage was already removed in #3571593. Both are scheduled for removal in Drupal 13.0.0. Contrib modules using either function have until Drupal 13 to adapt.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: system_sort_themes() and system_check_directory() in system.module are deprecated in Drupal 11.4.0 with no replacement and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0.

Technical details

system_sort_themes($a, $b) sorted the installed-themes list on the themes administration page, placing the default theme first then sorting the rest case-insensitively by name. It was only ever called in one core location, SystemController::themesPage(). The fix inlines the identical logic as a static function closure passed directly to uasort(), then adds the standard @trigger_error deprecation notice to the original function body. system_check_directory($form_element, $form_state) was a form after_build callback that validated a configured directory path and set a form error when the path was invalid. Its last core call (from the translation settings form) was removed in #3571593. The function body is preserved verbatim; only the @trigger_error call and PHPDoc @deprecated tag are added. Both functions reference change record https://www.drupal.org/node/3566774 and are marked deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed from drupal:13.0.0 with no replacement. This task is part of the broader system module procedural-function cleanup; #3015812 handles the related system_region_list() and system_default_region() deprecations.

Upgrade

Contrib modules calling system_sort_themes() should inline the sorting logic directly:

// Before
uasort($themes, 'system_sort_themes');

// After
uasort($themes, static function ($a, $b) {
  if ($a->is_default) {
    return -1;
  }
  if ($b->is_default) {
    return 1;
  }
  return strcasecmp($a->info['name'], $b->info['name']);
});

Contrib modules using system_check_directory() as a form after_build callback should remove the callback and reimplement any directory-validation logic they need directly in their form or form submission handler. There is no drop-in replacement.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 3, 2026
  • Committed: April 27, 2026 (2 months and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3526560: Mark Umami as hidden in preparation of moving to contrib and/or a testing profile
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +1/−0 · yml +1
  • Diff: 03eb694
  • Discussion: #3526560 · 8 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

The Umami Food Magazine demo profile is now hidden from Drupal's installer profile selection screen. It remains in core so that the many existing tests that depend on it continue to work, but site builders will no longer encounter it when setting up a new site. This is a preparatory step toward moving Umami to a contributed site template, as Drupal CMS and its site templates have superseded it as the recommended demo experience.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The entire change is a single line added to profiles/demo_umami/demo_umami.info.yml: hidden: true. Setting this key in a profile's info file causes Drupal's installer to skip the profile during the profile-selection step, while still allowing the profile to be installed programmatically (as tests do). The issue is explicitly framed as an interim step before the larger work tracked in #3523324, which proposes deprecating and removing Umami from core entirely in favour of a contributed site template. As catch noted in the thread, combined with #3159848 (merging minimal and standard profiles into a recipe), this change means core can be installed from the UI without presenting any profile selection at all.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 26, 2025
  • Committed: April 27, 2026 (11 months later)

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3512962: Add validation to field.storage.*.* indexes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3512962 · 8 contributors · 42 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Field storage index definitions in field.storage.*.* configuration previously used type: ignore in the config schema, meaning their structure was never validated. This change adds a FieldStorageIndexes config schema constraint that validates the shape of the indexes array: each entry must have a non-empty string key (the index name), and each column definition within that entry must be either a non-empty string (a bare column name) or a two-element array of [column_name, positive_integer_length]. This matches the format required by the Database Schema API and enforced at the database level. Existing valid field storage configs are unaffected.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New FieldStorageIndexes config schema constraint validates the structure of field.storage.*.* index definitions against the Database Schema API's expected array shape.

Technical details

The indexes key in field.storage.*.* (in field.schema.yml) had its inner sequence typed as type: ignore, providing zero structural validation. The fix adds a FieldStorageIndexes: ~ constraint declaration directly on the outer indexes sequence.

Two new classes are added under Drupal\field\Plugin\Validation\Constraint. FieldStorageIndexesConstraint extends SymfonyConstraint and is tagged with #[Constraint(id: 'FieldStorageIndexes', type: ['sequence'])]. Its validator FieldStorageIndexesConstraintValidator enforces:

  1. NULL or empty array passes (the field is optional).
  2. The value must be an array; non-arrays trigger $constraint->message.
  3. Each key must be a non-empty string (index name); otherwise invalidIndexNameMessage.
  4. Each value must be a PHP list array (column definitions); otherwise invalidIndexMessage.
  5. Each column definition is valid if it is a non-empty string, or a two-element list [column_name, length] where column_name is a non-empty string and length is a positive integer; other shapes trigger invalidColumnMessage or invalidColumnLengthMessage.

This shape exactly mirrors what Schema::addIndex() documents and what mysql\Driver\Database\mysql\Schema::createKeySql() implements: each column is either a bare name string or a (name, length) tuple. The (string, int) tuple cannot be expressed declaratively in config schema type definitions alone, which is why a custom constraint is required rather than a purely schema-driven approach (confirmed by longwave in the commit comment).

A kernel test FieldStorageIndexesValidationTest (in Drupal\Tests\field\Kernel\Config) covers a wide matrix of valid and invalid inputs. Index name and column name content are intentionally not validated against actual database column lists, as that was considered out of scope. The MappingCollectionConstraint introduced in #3535734 was considered but a more focused custom constraint was chosen for simplicity.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 14, 2025
  • Committed: April 27, 2026 (1 year and 2 commits later)

Collaboration

The issue stalled for months as bbrala, borisson_, and smustgrave repeatedly debated whether the indexes format was too flexible to validate meaningfully. The turning point came when nod_ resurfaced the thread with an updated search of ->setIndexes( usage across contrib, prompting a fresh read. godotislate then dug into Schema::addIndex() and mysql\Schema::createKeySql() to precisely define the valid column definition format, establishing that each column is either a bare string or a two-element [name, length] array with a positive integer length. That specificity gave bbrala the clear target needed to implement a focused custom constraint rather than chasing a fully generic solution. borisson_ requested one additional strictness adjustment before approving, and godotislate confirmed the result looked correct.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3393274: The theme must be passed as a query argument
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal support request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3393274 · 23 contributors · 52 comments
  • Followers: 57

Summary

After upgrading to Drupal 10.1.x, site owners began seeing frequent log entries reading "The theme must be passed as a query argument" from AssetControllerBase::deliver(). The errors appear as HTTP 400 responses against aggregated CSS/JS file URLs. Investigation across many sites and environments confirmed this is expected behavior: the errors are produced when clients request aggregated asset URLs that lack the required query parameters, which happens due to stale pages held in reverse-proxy caches (Cloudflare, Varnish), browsers with old tabs open from pre-10.1 visits, bots such as GPTBot crawling old URLs, or certain contrib modules (LiteSpeed Cache, Simple Menu Icons, bandwidth optimization modules) generating or interfering with asset URLs. The issue was closed without a code change. A separately resolved issue (#3377310) had already fixed the related problem of these correct 400 responses being incorrectly logged through the PHP error channel.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Drupal 10.1.x redesigned asset aggregation so that aggregated CSS/JS files can be regenerated on demand via a controller. AssetControllerBase::deliver() requires theme, delta, language, and include query parameters to know which aggregate to rebuild. When a request arrives for a path under sites/default/files/css/ or sites/default/files/js/ but those parameters are absent or malformed (e.g. double-encoded &amp; instead of &), the controller throws a BadRequestHttpException because it has no way to determine what aggregate to generate. This is intentional: without the parameters the URL is structurally unresolvable. Before 10.1.x, Drupal 9's AssetResolver::getCssAssets() derived the active theme directly from ThemeManager::getActiveTheme() without needing URL-encoded context, so old D9-era asset URLs naturally lack these parameters. Common real-world sources of these requests include: clients loading pages cached by Varnish or Cloudflare from before the upgrade, browsers pre-fetching assets from cached page state, AI crawlers (GPTBot) following stale links, and contrib modules that store or rewrite asset URLs. Catch confirmed in the thread that a 301 redirect workaround is not feasible because the parameters are the only source of information needed to construct the redirect target. The issue was ultimately closed as "works as designed".

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 11, 2023

Key contributors

  • cilefen
  • cellear
  • calebtr
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • darchuletajr (Symmetri Technology)
  • jfauske (Ramsalt Lab)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
